mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and easing pressure on equity multiples, so unchanged or lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day SPX forward returns are not higher when FEDFUNDS is flat-to-down than when it is rising, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in retail sales, measured by RSAFS, indicates stronger consumer demand and revenue growth expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the expansion is already visible.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises by at least 1.0% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce negative or zero median 20-day SPX returns, or if positive-return frequency is not above 55% versus the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals labor market deterioration and future earnings risk, so an increase in unemployment should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the effect reverses for two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and PAYEMS both rise together, the economy is expanding through real production and payroll gains, which should support earnings breadth and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO/PAYEMS improvements do not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of joint-strength episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling CPIAUCSL inflation and a stable or lower FEDFUNDS rate reduces real-rate pressure and supports valuation expansion, creating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation episodes without policy easing fail to improve 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive and significant in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after a prior housing peak signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and slowing cyclical growth, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice growth risk quickly.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 5% from its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-drop triggers do not lead to negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the effect disappears after controlling for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 after contraction indicates improving final demand and earnings expectations, so positive quarterly GDP momentum should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 prints positive quarter-over-quarter growth after a prior weak or negative quarter
failure_condition: Reject if post-contraction GDP accelerations fail to outperform the unconditional 60-day return rate, or if revisions remove the sign of the effect in historical real-time data.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, labor-market momentum is deteriorating beyond normal noise, and that joint weakening should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS slowdown and UNRATE increase does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered events show positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases.
